title: pulsatilla alpina subsp. austroalpina
intro: Pulsatilla alpina subsp. austroalpina, also known as the Alpine Pasque Flower, is a perennial plant native to the alpine regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, as well as its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system. The health benefits of Pulsatilla alpina subsp. austroalpina include relief from menstrual cramps, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its use in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant typically include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the dried flowers and roots in hot water.
